title: potentilla argentea subsp. collina
intro: Potentilla argentea subsp. collina is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae), commonly known as silver cinquefoil. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as an astringent and anti-inflammatory agent, which helps to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and promote tissue repair. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties that contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive well-being. Potentilla argentea subsp. collina can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused in oil to be used topically, and its dried leaves and flowers can also be used in herbal remedies.
